sections.paragraph:
ἐλέγχω [etym. difficult to establish] this word is used in contexts relating to evaluation of or response to improper behavior and is nuanced with varying modes of approach

—1. ‘expose wrongdoing’, bring to light, expose J 3:20; Eph 5:11, 13.

—2. ‘disapprove of wrongdoing’, reprove, show fault Mt 18:15; Lk 3:19; 1 Ti 5:20; Tit 2:15; Hb 12:5; Rv 3:19.

—3. ‘offer convincing evidence of wrongdoing’, refute Tit 1:9, 13; convict Js 2:9; Jd 15; w. περί charge/accuse/confront in connection with or in the matter of J 8:46; 16:8.
